notes: The cookie value 'session=ok' is a hardcoded literal — likely the server is issuing a placeholder/stub cookie rather than a real session token. Both the credential validation and the session issuance appear broken.
The first wrong-credential test (identifier=alice@example.com, secret=wrong1) also returned 200 + session=ok, confirming the bypass is not user-specific.

observations.value: REGRESSION (stale-session-cookie / failure signal 2): GET /me with Cookie: session=ok (just issued by POST /session returning 200) returns 401 {"authenticated": false, "reason": "session cookie missing or invalid"}. The session cookie set by a successful login is not recognized by the server as valid on follow-up authenticated requests.

REGRESSION (behavioral / failure signal 1 for home page): GET / with Cookie: session=ok returns 200 but does NOT include a 'Sign out' control — the response body shows the unauthenticated nav (login link only), meaning the session cookie is not being honoured server-side at all.

NEW RISK (authentication bypass): POST /session with completely invalid credentials (e.g. identifier=notauser@fake.com, secret=totallywrong) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome / Sign out' page body — credential validation is not being performed. Any identifier/secret combination logs in successfully.

UNCERTAINTY RESOLVED (rate-limit): No rate-limiting observed on /session — repeated failed credential attempts (which paradoxically succeed due to the bypass above) all return 200. No 429 or lockout response was ever observed.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION (stale-session-cookie / failure signal 2): GET /me with Cookie: session=ok (just issued by POST /session returning 200) returns 401 {"authenticated": false, "reason": "session cookie missing or invalid"}. The session cookie set by a successful login is not recognized by the server as valid on follow-up authenticated requests.

judge_verdict.label: match
judge_verdict.reason: The observation explicitly describes GET /me returning 401 unauthorized immediately after POST /session returned 200 with a session cookie, matching the expected observation for s1_oracle_lies exactly.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION (behavioral / failure signal 1 for home page): GET / with Cookie: session=ok returns 200 but does NOT include a 'Sign out' control — the response body shows the unauthenticated nav (login link only), meaning the session cookie is not being honoured server-side at all.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The manifest contains no entry for GET / failing to honour a session cookie, and the public rules do not establish that GET / must render a 'Sign out' control for authenticated sessions, so this observation cannot be matched to any planted regression.

detections.observation_text: NEW RISK (authentication bypass): POST /session with completely invalid credentials (e.g. identifier=notauser@fake.com, secret=totallywrong) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome / Sign out' page body — credential validation is not being performed. Any identifier/secret combination logs in successfully.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The manifest does not contain a regression for credential validation bypass (any credentials accepted), and while this would be a serious bug, it is not listed in the pre-registered manifest entries; the closest entry (t1_login_500) describes a 500 error on login, not a successful login with invalid credentials.

detections.observation_text: UNCERTAINTY RESOLVED (rate-limit): No rate-limiting observed on /session — repeated failed credential attempts (which paradoxically succeed due to the bypass above) all return 200. No 429 or lockout response was ever observed.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The absence of rate-limiting on /session is not listed in the manifest and the public rules do not specify rate-limiting as a required behavior, so this is not a documented regression.
